commands.repair_reason: - [P1] This PR needs maintainer proof acceptance and product-contract judgment, not an automated repair branch.; Cleared: No concrete new security defect was found on live head, but merge still needs maintainer acceptance because the example manages SSH keys and endpoint publication. (sha=05b61bfb77c45a6cb66425dbac925c000f959a40)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Slurm academic sandbox guidance, links it from existing docs, and introduces a reference `provider: external` Slurm adapter, sample unprivileged SSH runner, fake-Slurm tests, and Python cache ignores.

Reproducibility: not applicable. This PR adds a new Slurm external-provider docs/example path rather than fixing broken current behavior. The merge-risk proof path is clear: run the actual adapter on the live head with fake or live Slurm commands through doctor, acquire, list, release, and validation-failure cleanup.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This PR needs maintainer proof acceptance and product-contract judgment, not an automated repair branch.

target.title: docs: add Slurm academic sandbox plan and adapter
target.body:
Closes https://github.com/openclaw/crabbox/issues/325

## Summary

Adds a campus Slurm external-provider reference path without putting cluster-specific scheduler, account, filesystem, gateway, or security policy in Crabbox core.

- Documents the recommended Slurm shape as `provider: external`.
- Adds `examples/slurm-external-provider/slurm-cbx.py`, a conservative adapter for `doctor`, `acquire`, `resolve`, `list`, `release`, `touch`, and `cleanup`.
- Adds `runner-unprivileged-sshd.sh` as a sample allocation-side SSH endpoint publisher.
- Adds fake-Slurm pytest coverage for acquire/resolve/release, idempotency, timeout rollback, immediate `sbatch` rejection rollback, unknown scheduler state, failed cancellation, list filtering, and cleanup.
- Fixes immediate `sbatch` submission failures so non-keep acquires remove the per-lease local state/key directory before any job ID exists.

## Verification

Local validation on current head `05b61bfb77c45a6cb66425dbac925c000f959a40`:

```text
python3 -m py_compile examples/slurm-external-provider/slurm-cbx.py examples/slurm-external-provider/test_slurm_cbx.py
PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1 python3 -m pytest examples/slurm-external-provider -q
node scripts/check-command-docs.mjs
node scripts/check-docs-links.mjs
bash -n examples/slurm-external-provider/runner-unprivileged-sshd.sh
git diff --check
```

Result: 25 fake-Slurm pytest tests passed.

Public CI is green on current head: https://github.com/openclaw/crabbox/actions/runs/28100581771

## Representative Protocol Proof

Representative external-provider proof was rerun on current head `05b61bfb77c45a6cb66425dbac925c000f959a40` against the actual adapter with fake `sbatch`/`squeue`/`sacct`/`scancel` binaries on PATH, no real campus credentials required:

```text
PATH=<fake-slurm-bin>:$PATH \
  examples/slurm-external-provider/slurm-cbx.py \
  --state-dir <tmp>/state \
  --runner-script <tmp>/runner.sh \
  --poll-interval 0
```

Proof summary:

```text
doctor -> protocolVersion=1, Slurm external provider ready
acquire -> lease cbx_slurmproof01, cloudId slurm/job/123456;cluster, status ready, SSH host node123.cluster.example.edu:39022
list -> one ready lease with slurmState RUNNING
release -> released Slurm lease, fake scheduler called scancel 123456
list after release -> []
```

Full redacted output is posted here: https://github.com/openclaw/crabbox/pull/345#issuecomment-4789756522

## Merge Caveat

This is representative proof, not live campus Slurm proof. Do not merge unless maintainers accept representative proof as sufficient for this P3 external-adapter docs/example contract; live campus proof remains stronger.
target.author: zozo123

commands.repair_reason: - No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed; this maintainer-authored, protected-label PR needs maintainer review and required checks rather than an automated fix.; Cleared: The diff adds a fixed-argument local git rev-parse probe for QA metadata and does not change dependencies, workflows, permissions, secrets, or package resolution. (sha=c79fb2cddd720125c829ae3301e8eacd3f8cfeac)
commands.review_summary:
The branch centralizes QA evidence ref resolution and threads `repoRoot` through suite, test-file, live-transport, and UX Matrix evidence producers so artifacts record the checked-out ref before falling back to `GITHUB_SHA`.

PR surface: Source +47, Tests +24, Other +8. Total +79 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source and PR proof: current main records `OPENCLAW_QA_REF || GITHUB_SHA`, so an inherited event SHA can differ from the checked-out repo under test. I did not run local tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed; this maintainer-authored, protected-label PR needs maintainer review and required checks rather than an automated fix.
